title_zh: 亿万富翁的阴道俱乐部

paragraphs.en: Brave New World Dept.
paragraphs.zh: 《勇敢新世界》栏目

paragraphs.en: The Billionaires’ Vagina Club
paragraphs.zh: 亿万富翁的阴道俱乐部

paragraphs.en: With her motto, “Sexual health is health,” Dr. Sally Greenwald aims to optimize orgasms for the women of Silicon Valley.
paragraphs.zh: 萨莉·格林沃尔德博士以“性健康就是健康”为座右铭，致力于为硅谷女性优化性高潮体验。

paragraphs.en: By Melanie Thernstrom
paragraphs.zh: 梅兰妮·瑟恩斯特罗姆 撰文

paragraphs.en: Greenwald focusses on what she calls a woman’s “sexspan.” She tells her patients, “If having orgasms in your eighties is on your bucket list, you have to work at it.”Photo illustration by Sarah Illenberger
paragraphs.zh: 格林沃尔德将重点放在她称之为女性“性功能黄金期”上。她对病人说：“如果你希望自己八十多岁还能高潮，那你必须为此付出努力。”（插图作者：萨拉·伊伦伯格）

paragraphs.en: Sally Greenwald likes to say that she is the happiest gynecologist in America. She is certainly sought after, having attracted a following of high-powered Silicon Valley women of a certain age with her motto, “Sexual health is health.” Recently, Greenwald was the guest of honor at a small luncheon hosted by one of her patients, the tech entrepreneur and philanthropist Gisel Kordestani, in Atherton, California, one of the wealthiest towns in the country. The event took place in the stately, pillared home Kordestani shares with her husband, Omid, the former chairman of Twitter.
paragraphs.zh: 莎莉·格林沃尔德喜欢说自己是美国最快乐的妇产科医生。她确实很受欢迎，因为她以“性健康就是健康”这句口号吸引了一批有着一定年龄的高层硅谷女性追随者。最近，她被其一位病人、科技创业者兼慈善家吉塞尔·科德斯塔尼（Gisel Kordestani）在加州阿瑟顿这座全国最富裕城镇之一的宴会中当作贵宾邀请。活动在科德斯塔尼与丈夫奥米德（Omid）共住的那座端庄、有柱廊的住宅里举行。奥米德是推特前任主席。

paragraphs.en: Kordestani, who has long, light-brown hair and was wearing platform Prada sneakers, apologized that her co-host, a fellow Greenwald patient named Nicole Lacob, who runs the philanthropic foundation of the Golden State Warriors (her husband, Joe, is the majority owner), wasn’t able to come at the last minute. She had flown to Paris to attend the début runway show of Matthieu Blazy, the new designer for Chanel.
paragraphs.zh: 科德斯塔尼有一头很长的浅棕色头发，她穿着普拉达的厚底运动鞋，并向大家道歉，表示她的共同主持人——另一位格林沃尔德患者、也是金州勇士慈善基金会负责人妮可·拉科布（Nicole Lacob）临时不能出席。她临时飞往巴黎，去参加马蒂厄·布拉齐（Matthieu Blazy）担任沙龙时装总监后的首次发布会。

paragraphs.en: The other women in the room murmured in understanding. Ranging in age from late thirties to mid-fifties, they were accomplished, confident—the kind of women for whom “Fake it till you make it” had long ago morphed into “Make it better and better.” When I moved to Silicon Valley from New York, fifteen years ago, I was astonished by the widespread conviction that everything—even one’s body, aging and mortality notwithstanding—must and will improve, like each new iteration of iPhone.
paragraphs.zh: 屋里其他女性都懂地轻声附和。她们年龄从三十多岁末到五十多岁中段不等，成就斐然、神情自信——她们是那种很早就把“你要装作擅长，直到你真正做得好”这句老话，变成“不断把它做得更好”的人。十五年前我从纽约搬到硅谷时，我就惊讶于这种广泛的信念：一切都必须、也都会不断进步，即使是人的身体，尽管衰老和死亡终将来临，它也像新一代 iPhone 一样不断升级。

paragraphs.en: Greenwald, who is forty and has a spirited demeanor, perched on the edge of a chair, ready to field questions. “Use my brain!” she told the women, her high blond ponytail bouncing. “Meander me wherever you want to go.” The doctor, who grew up nearby, in Los Altos, fit in easily with the group. Her sparkling Jimmy Choo flats peeked out from under a long, rainbow-striped dress, and her language immediately signalled that she shares Silicon Valley’s obsession with optimization and innovation—“perseveration on perfection,” she calls it, a determination to bring the entrepreneurial mind-set into every aspect of one’s life. The women at the lunch were not content to enjoy good health; they were looking for a way to level up. (The latest term for this is “healthmaxxing.”)
paragraphs.zh: 格林沃尔德今年四十岁，性格爽朗，坐在椅子边缘，随时准备回答问题。她向这些女性们说：“用我的大脑吧！”高高的金色马尾在颤动，“你想把我带到哪儿都行。”这位在洛斯阿尔托斯附近长大的医生很快就和这群人混得来。她那双闪亮的Jimmy Choo平底鞋从一条长长的彩虹条纹连衣裙下隐约露出，语言里立刻流露出她对硅谷对“优化”和“创新”痴迷的认同——她把这称作“对完美的持续执着”，也就是把创业型思维带进生活的每一个方面。午餐会上的这些女性并不满足于仅仅拥有良好的健康状态；她们想要的是一种“升级”。（最近这种想法被称作“healthmaxxing”。）

paragraphs.en: One difference between Greenwald and the dozens of biohacking doctors who treat the area’s billionaire tech bros is that she wants to achieve this goal, in part, through orgasms. (Lube, she likes to say, is as essential to one’s routine as sunscreen.) Rather than concentrate on increasing life span, she focusses on what she calls a woman’s “sexspan.” Her concierge gynecology practice—which is situated on the Stanford campus, where she is an assistant clinical professor—views women’s sexual health as a way to well-being and, ultimately, to longevity. She makes the case that “bro science” culture typically relies on research that excludes data about women. (Rock-star longevity doctors like Peter Attia and Andrew Huberman come to mind.) Part of Greenwald’s approach involves using cutting-edge hormone therapy, among other interventions, in unconventional ways. She also counsels women to have more and better sex, devising stratagems like Fuck-It February, a prescription to have sex as often as possible in the year’s shortest month. New patients pay tens of thousands of dollars a year to join her practice, if they manage to get off of a wait list. Some have sought Greenwald out after hearing her speak at private gatherings hosted by fans like Priscilla Chan Zuckerberg. Last fall, after a fierce auction, Greenwald received a seven-figure book advance from HarperCollins. Sheryl Sandberg is writing a blurb.
paragraphs.zh: 格林沃尔德与那些治疗湾区亿万富翁男性“科技老兄”群体的数十位生物黑客医生不同在于，她希望部分地通过性高潮来实现这一目标。（她常说，润滑剂和防晒霜一样，是任何人的日常必需品。）她与其强调延长寿命，不如把重点放在她所称的女性“性寿命”上。她在斯坦福校园内（担任助理临床教授）开设的会所式妇科实践，将女性性健康视作通往健康福祉，最终通向长寿的路径。她认为，“bro科学”文化通常依赖于排除了女性数据的研究。（人们想到的就有彼得·阿蒂亚和安德鲁·霍博曼这类长寿领域的“明星医生”。）格林沃尔德的方法之一，是以非传统方式运用最前沿的激素疗法等干预。她还建议女性进行更多、更高质量的性爱，并设计了像“随它去二月（Fuck-It February）”这样的策略：在一年中最短的一个月里尽可能频繁地发生性关系。新病人若能脱离候诊名单，每年要支付数万美元才能加入她的诊所。有些人是听说她在普里西拉·陈·扎克伯格（Priscilla Chan Zuckerberg）等粉丝举办的私人聚会上发言后才去找她的。去年秋天，经过一场激烈竞拍，格林沃尔德从HarperCollins拿到了一笔七位数的书稿预付款。谢丽尔·桑德伯格正在为她写背书。

paragraphs.en: Sandberg described Greenwald to me as “brilliant, energetic, passionate,” adding, “She’s unafraid to broach topics in ways that other people, I think, have been afraid to do.” In the past few years, as her wait list blew up, Greenwald started jokingly referring to her practice as the B.V.C.—the Billionaires’ Vagina Club. Some patients found the name amusing, but the billionairesses apparently did not. Greenwald stopped using it.
paragraphs.zh: 桑德伯格当时对我说，格林沃尔德“聪明、精力充沛、充满激情”，并补充说：“她敢于以我认为别人不敢碰的方式去触及这些话题。”近几年，随着她的预约名单爆满，格林沃尔德开始半开玩笑地把自己的诊所叫作B.V.C.—“亿万富翁阴道俱乐部”。有些病人觉得这个名字很有趣，但那些女亿万富豪显然并不买账。于是她停用了这个称呼。

paragraphs.en: Seated in Kordestani’s grand living room, the lunch guests told Greenwald about their frustration with their primary-care doctors, whom they viewed as old-fashioned disease chasers. “I like biohacking,” Kordestani said, but complained that her doctors were dismissive. The women wanted physicians who were disease preventers, and doctors who were attentive to the difficulties wrought by menopause, which have been increasingly found to leave women vulnerable to other ailments. (I first met Kordestani at a book group that was reading “All Fours,” Miranda July’s 2024 novel about a woman teetering on the estrogen cliff of perimenopause, ditching her husband and child in the process. The book-club members roundly disliked it, indignant that the character was being driven mad by perimenopause, as if hormone-replacement therapy didn’t exist. Did July just have a bad gynecologist?)
paragraphs.zh: 在科代斯塔尼（Kordestani）的宏大客厅里，午餐来宾向格林沃尔德抱怨自己对家庭医生的不满，认为这些医生思维陈旧，只会追着疾病跑。“我喜欢生物黑客，”科代斯塔尼说，但她抱怨自己的医生太不在意。女性们希望有的是“预防疾病”的医生，而不是“治已病”的医生；她们也希望医生关注更早更少被重视的更年期困扰，而这类困扰被发现会让女性对其他疾病更易感。 （我第一次见到科代斯塔尼，是在一个读书会，大家正在读米兰达·贾列（Miranda July）的2024年小说《All Fours》。书里写的是一个在围绝经期“雌激素悬崖”边缘摇摆的女性，过程中与丈夫和孩子分别。书友会成员一致不喜欢这本书，愤怒地认为，主角是被围绝经期逼疯了，仿佛激素替代疗法并不存在。难道六月只是遇到了一位糟糕的妇科医生？）

paragraphs.en: Outside Kordestani’s glass doors, a gigantic inflatable flamingo moved back and forth in an infinity pool. Inside, one young woman asked Greenwald, “How do I know when I’m in perimenopause?”
paragraphs.zh: 在科德斯塔尼那扇玻璃门外，一只巨大的充气火烈鸟在无边泳池里往复移动。室内，一位年轻女士问格林瓦尔德：“我怎么知道自己进入更年期过渡期了？”

paragraphs.en: “My goal is that my patients never know,” Greenwald answered, segueing to her enthusiasm for hormones. She told the group that she had several Olympic athletes as patients. As soon as their schedules for the 2024 Paris Games were released, she used an ovulation medication to synch their cycles so that they would compete on days nine through fourteen—the time when estrogen peaks and women are suffused with energy. In perimenopause, she said, the pattern shifts: fluctuating hormones can cause days and days of angsty P.M.S., followed by spikes of anxiety. “Fortysomething women can get quite a reputation,” she noted. “These are badass women like yourselves, who run companies, run households with a lot of children, have M.B.A. careers and whatnot, and all of a sudden they’re freaking out at everyone.
paragraphs.zh: “我的目标是让我的患者永远不要知道，”格林瓦尔德回答道，顺势表达了她对激素的热情。她告诉小组，她的病人里有几位奥运会运动员。她在2024年巴黎奥运会赛程一公布后，就给她们用了排卵药物，让她们的月经周期同步，以便她们都在比赛日的第九到第十四天参赛——也就是雌激素达到高峰、女性精力充沛的时候。她说，在更年期过渡期，这种模式会改变：激素波动会导致连续几天令人烦躁的经前综合征（P.M.S.），随后又接连出现焦虑高峰。“四十多岁的女性有时会留下相当‘出名’的名声，”她说。“你们这些人就是这样的厉害女性，既经营公司，也操持有很多孩子的家庭，有 MBA 的职业经历等等，结果突然一下子对所有人都开始抓狂。”

paragraphs.en: “I’m super excited about using hormones, maybe forever,” Greenwald went on. “I will humbly bow my head when the data come out to prove the opposite, but I really think we’re onto something.”
paragraphs.zh: “我对使用激素充满期待，也许会持续一生，”格林瓦尔德接着说。“如果数据显示相反，我会谦卑地低头承认，但我真的觉得我们正在摸到一条真路。”

paragraphs.en: When she is talking about scientific innovations, Greenwald warns her listeners that she is prone to “go super dorky.” Although the women she treats are data-driven, they are impatient—unwilling to wait a decade for randomized controlled trials, then years more for prescribing habits to change and insurance companies to catch up. She makes treatment recommendations based on a combination of what she calls “biological plausibility”—a theory of why something would work—and new data. “There’s a lot of cool new meds on the market that I’m super excited about that few doctors are using,” she said. “But I am!
paragraphs.zh: 每当她谈到科学创新时，格林瓦尔德都会提醒听众说自己容易“变得特别‘书呆’”。她治疗的女性是以数据为导向的，但她们又很急进——不愿意等十年去看随机对照试验结果，再等几年等待方针开出处方的改变，等保险公司跟上脚步。她基于自己所说的“生物学合理性”（即某种疗法为何有效的理论依据）与最新数据相结合来给出治疗建议。“市场上有很多很酷的新药我非常兴奋，但很少有医生在用，”她说，“可我在用！”

paragraphs.en: “I want to be very clear about when I’m speaking as a U.C.S.F.-trained physician with lots of randomized control trials behind what I’m saying, versus when I’ve entered the ‘woo-woo’ world of concierge medicine and I’m going into anecdotal evidence and observational studies,” she said, fluttering her hands. “You guys are a very smart room here.”
paragraphs.zh: “我想很明确地说清楚：当我以一位受过U.C.S.F.训练、拥有大量随机对照试验（RCT）背景的医生身份发言时，这是一个层面；而当我走进‘神秘玄学’式的会所式医疗世界时，我说的是轶事证据和观察性研究。”她一边说一边摆了摆手。“你们这儿都是非常聪明的人。”

paragraphs.en: Cartoon by John O’Brien
paragraphs.zh: 约翰·奥布莱恩的漫画

paragraphs.en: A woman mentioned that some of her friends were paying for their young daughters to freeze their eggs, as a graduation present.
paragraphs.zh: 一位女士提到，她的一些朋友把给自己小女儿冻卵当作毕业礼物在资助。

paragraphs.en: “Super interesting! That’s a great idea!” Greenwald said. “This is my favorite example of population-based health versus concierge health.” Research shows that most women who freeze their eggs before the age of thirty-seven don’t end up using them. “But suppose we don’t care what the cost is?” Greenwald asked. The calculus becomes simpler. Everyone knows twenty-one-year-old eggs are better.
paragraphs.zh: “太有意思了！这真是个好主意！”格林瓦尔德说。“这是我最喜欢的人群基准健康观 versus 会所式健康观的例子。研究表明，大多数在三十七岁前冻卵的女性最终并不会用上那些卵子。”“但假设我们不在乎成本呢？”格林瓦尔德问道。这样一来，算式就简单了。大家都知道，二十一岁的卵子更好。”

paragraphs.en: Cori Bates, a trustee of the Asian Art Museum who is married to the C.E.O. of the software company Genesys, leaned forward. Bates was wearing a scarlet dress and kitten-heeled flip-flops, and she spoke up to say that, after learning that she was at a high risk for ovarian cancer, she funded a study to assess whether risk for the disease can be reduced by removing one’s fallopian tubes.
paragraphs.zh: 亚洲艺术博物馆的理事科里·贝茨与软件公司 Genesys 的首席执行官结婚。她微微探身前倾，身着猩红色连衣裙，脚踩小猫跟人字拖，开口说在得知自己患卵巢癌风险很高后，她资助了一项研究，旨在评估通过切除输卵管是否能降低该疾病的风险。

paragraphs.en: Greenwald praised the study, and Bates took the opportunity to ask if the doctor would take her as a patient. Greenwald beamed: “I’m so, so flattered. I would love to. But I’m completely full.” She promised to try to find room for Bates in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德赞扬了这项研究，贝茨随即借机询问她是否愿意收我为病人。格林瓦尔德笑得很开心：“我真的非常荣幸，我当然愿意，但我现在已经排满了。”她承诺以后会尽量给贝茨腾出空位。

paragraphs.en: Someone asked Greenwald how she felt about hormone-monitoring apps, and she answered that the technology is not there yet and that the apps on the market are unreliable. She addressed her audience: “Please, make a good app for teen-age girls—and for perimenopause and menopause.” The women smiled, then everyone filed into the dining room. Greenwald left before dessert so that she could be home in time to meet three of her four sons at their bus stop.
paragraphs.zh: 有人问格林瓦尔德她如何看待激素监测应用程序，她回答说这项技术还不够成熟，市面上的应用也不可靠。她对大家说：“请你们做一个真正好用的青春期女孩应用——也要有适合围绝经期和绝经期女性使用的。”女士们笑了，接着大家都陆续走向餐厅。格林瓦尔德在甜点上桌前离开了，这样才能赶回家，在三个儿子的公交站接他们。

paragraphs.en: I met Greenwald three years ago, when she was consulting once a week for the primary-care practice where I was a patient. She strode into the room looking like a television doctor in a chic blue-print dress and flats. (She favors prints, she told me, because they disguise splashes of bodily fluid.) By then, I was skeptical of gynecologists. I had consulted three about insomnia, brain fog, and vaginal atrophy, which made sex painful and left me with recurrent infections, only to be told that the ill effects of menopause are just a “natural stage of life” which could not be medicated away. Then the doctors would bustle out to see their more appealing patients—the cheerily pregnant ones, whose deliveries constituted the bulk of their income and whose babies would be added to the collage of photos decorating their office walls. Greenwald told me that, even though I had been prescribed some hormones, she felt that I had been undertreated, because doctors often use hormones too sparingly; she prescribed five different forms of hormones to me, and my old life resumed.
paragraphs.zh: 三年前，我第一次见到格林瓦尔德，当时她每周都会到我就诊的全科门诊为患者提供咨询。她一进房间就像一位电视节目里的医生，穿着一件时髦的蓝图印花连衣裙和芭蕾平底鞋。（她告诉我，她偏爱印花图案，因为能掩盖体液飞溅。）那时我对妇科医生已经很怀疑了。我曾向三位医生咨询失眠、脑雾和阴道萎缩，这些问题让性生活变得痛苦，并让我反复感染，却被告知更年期的不适只是“自然的人生阶段”，无法用药改善。之后医生们就会匆忙去看那些更受欢迎的孕妇患者——因为她们的分娩带来的收入是主要来源，而且她们的宝宝会被放进办公室墙上的照片拼贴里。格林瓦尔德告诉我，虽然我已经被开了些激素药，她仍觉得我的治疗明显不足，因为医生常常对激素治疗过于保守；于是她给我开了五种不同剂型的激素，我的旧生活也重新开始了。

paragraphs.en: I texted my middle-aged friends the details of my prescriptions: estrogen patch (estradiol, 0.075 mg.), vaginal ring (Estring, 7.5 mg.), estrogen cream (estradiol, 0.01 per cent), oral progesterone (Prometrium, 100 mg.), compounded testosterone cream (start at 1 mg., then move to 2 mg.—up to 10 mg.). They pressed their own doctors, who hedged or shrugged but eventually wrote the prescriptions for them. When my friends found relief, they were thrilled—and infuriated. Why were they getting this information from me and not their doctors?
paragraphs.zh: 我把自己的处方明细发给了几位中年朋友：雌激素透皮贴片（雌二醇，0.075 毫克）、阴道环（伊曲斯特林？不，这里是 Estring，7.5 毫克）、雌激素阴道霜（雌二醇，0.01%）、口服黄体酮（Prometrium，100 毫克）、复方睾酮霜（先从 1 毫克开始，再增加到 2 毫克——最高可达 10 毫克）。她们去催促自己的医生，医生起初要么回避要么耸肩，但最终还是给她们开了这些处方。朋友们一旦得到了明显缓解，既欣喜又愤怒：为什么她们从我这里得到了这些信息，而不是从医生那里？

paragraphs.en: For many women, menopause marks a retirement from active sexuality. They may still have sex, in the way a retired athlete may still throw a ball around the back yard, but the drive is gone. Greenwald wants to get these women back into the game, because research shows that a healthy sex life brings a variety of benefits that can contribute to longevity. Her methods combine treatments with sex tips. “How many sexually active years do you want to have?” she asks patients. “If having orgasms in your eighties is on your bucket list, you have to work at it.” A faltering sex life can also put a marriage at risk, and statistics suggest that people who stay partnered live longer. In the 2024 General Social Survey, nearly two-thirds of married adults reported having sex less than once a week, and twenty-eight per cent answered that they had sex rarely or not at all.
paragraphs.zh: 对许多女性来说，绝经常被视为性活力退场的起点。她们可能仍然会做爱，就像一位退役运动员仍会在后院随手把球扔一扔一样，但性欲和冲动已经没了。格林沃尔德希望把这些女性重新带回赛场，因为研究表明，健康的性生活带来多种益处，并可能有助于长寿。她的方法是把治疗与性健康建议结合起来。她对患者说：“你想要有多少年的性生活？如果你希望在八十多岁还能高潮，那就得主动去做。” 性生活减退也会让婚姻面临风险，而统计数据显示，持续有伴侣关系的人寿命更长。在 2024 年《综合社会调查》中，近三分之二的已婚成年人表示他们每周性生活不足一次，而有 28% 的受访者称几乎没有性生活或完全没有。

paragraphs.en: Women’s sexual health has been under-researched, and in recent years federal research money into women’s health issues has been slashed. But menopause is finally having a moment. Last November, the F.D.A. removed the “black box” warning (the agency’s strictest safety alert) against H.R.T., and earlier this month Melinda French Gates announced a two-hundred-million-dollar menopause- research initiative. A 2024 meta-analysis published by the Bulletin of the World Health Organization looked at sixty-three studies and found strong correlations among over-all health, well-being, and sexual activity. A study from the same year in the Journal of Psychosexual Health analyzed government data and found that women who had low sexual frequency had a forty-six per cent higher mortality risk. It seems obvious that healthier people might be more sexually active, but Greenwald believes that the association is bidirectional: that good sex leads to good health. Orgasms improve mental health, suppressing stress hormones like cortisol and releasing dopamine and oxytocin; women with robust sex lives report less depression and anxiety. Studies show that orgasms also relieve menstrual pain, lower blood pressure, enhance circulation, correlate with better cardiac functioning, and burn calories equivalent to thirty minutes of brisk walking. Orgasms also activate parasympathetic pathways that promote deeper and longer sleep. There are measurable health benefits to orgasms through masturbation, but those benefits compound in partnered sex, which supports satisfying long-term relationships, which in turn correlate with better health and longevity.
paragraphs.zh: 女性性健康长期以来研究不足，近年联邦对女性健康议题的科研经费也被削减了。不过，绝经问题终于开始受到关注。去年 11 月，美国食品药品监督管理局（F.D.A.）取消了对激素替代疗法（H.R.T.）的“黑框警告”（该机构最严格的安全提示），而本月初，梅琳达·盖茨宣布了一项价值两亿美元的绝经研究倡议。同年，世界卫生组织《公报》发表的一项荟萃分析共纳入 63 项研究，发现总体健康、幸福感与性活动之间存在显著相关。同期在《性心理健康杂志》发表的一项研究分析了政府数据，发现性活动频率低的女性其死亡风险高出 46%。显然，健康的人可能更有性活动，但格林沃尔德认为这种关系是双向的：健康性生活也能促进健康。高潮可改善心理健康，抑制皮质醇等压力激素，并释放多巴胺和催产素；拥有稳定性经验的女性往往报告更少的抑郁和焦虑。研究表明，高潮还能缓解经期疼痛、降低血压、改善循环，与更好的心脏功能相关，并可消耗相当于快走三十分钟的热量。高潮还会激活副交感神经通路，从而带来更深、更长的睡眠。通过自慰也能获得可量化的健康益处，但在伴侣关系中的性生活会使这些效益叠加，因为它支持令人满足的长期关系，而长期关系本身又与更好的健康和更长寿命相关。

paragraphs.en: “The argument is not: sex equals longevity,” Greenwald said. “It is: sex equals sleep, stress reduction, cardiovascular engagement, relational fulfillment, and pelvic-floor health, and each of those equals health.” She likes to note, however, that sixty years after the so-called sexual revolution, the sex lives of straight women are hardly thriving. Whereas straight men climax in almost all sexual encounters, heterosexual women in relationships fail to climax a third of the time. The widespread assumption has always been that the female orgasm is innately tricky, but data show that lesbians climax almost as often as men do.
paragraphs.zh: 格林沃尔德说：“我的观点不是：性等于长寿。我的观点是：性意味着更好的睡眠、减压、心血管活动、关系满足，以及骨盆底健康，而这些都意味着健康。” 她也常提醒人们，如今在所谓性革命后六十年，异性恋女性的性生活仍谈不上兴旺。异性恋男性在几乎所有性接触中都会达到高潮，但处于异性恋关系中的女性三分之一时间未能达到高潮。长期以来，人们普遍认为女性高潮天生难以获得，但数据表明，女同性恋者达到高潮的频率几乎与男性一样高。

paragraphs.en: A number of Greenwald’s patients told me that they’d never had a gynecologist who thought of sexual satisfaction as a medical issue. But she considers sex coaching to be a part of her job, just as an internist might advise a patient with high blood pressure to get more exercise.
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德的许多病人告诉我，她从来没有遇到过把性满足当作医疗问题的妇科医生。但她认为性教育与性健康指导是自己工作的一部分，就像内科医生会建议高血压患者多做运动一样。

paragraphs.en: There is a girlfriendly “pass it on” quality to the way word has spread about Greenwald’s treatments, which include birth-control pills—such as Nextstellis and Natazia—that contain a new natural form of estrogen, not the synthetic formulation in most pills; compounded testosterone cream for a “kick-ass sex-drive booster”; magnesium glycinate for sleep; temperature-controlled mattresses; her preferred vibrator for postmenopausal women (a long-handled model with seventy-to-a-hundred-and-fifty-hertz frequencies); and silicone-based lube. Greenwald has been known to draw diagrams for patients, showing them how to find their G-spot.
paragraphs.zh: 关于格林瓦尔德的治疗方式在女性群体中传播开来时，带着一种“口口相传”的女性友好特质。她的治疗包括采用新型天然雌激素成分、而非多数避孕药里的合成配方的避孕药——比如Nextstellis和Natazia；为“性欲强力提升”而调配的复方睾酮霜；用于助眠的甘氨酸镁；温度可控床垫；她为更年期后的女性偏爱的按摩棒（长柄型号，频率范围为每秒七十到一百五十赫兹）；以及硅基润滑剂。格林瓦尔德还以给病人画图示闻名，她会示范如何寻找G点。

paragraphs.en: “Experts describe sex in biopsychosocial terms,” Greenwald told me. “I tell my patients, ‘Think bio.’ ”
paragraphs.zh: “专家会从生物—心理—社会三方面来解释性，”格林瓦尔德告诉我。“我则告诉我的病人，‘先想生物学。’”

paragraphs.en: Nicole Lacob, the wife of the Warriors owner, started seeing Greenwald over a year ago, when she was fifty-six—just in time, in terms of starting hormone-replacement therapy. (For women more than ten years into menopause, H.R.T. can boost mood, libido, and bone health, but it also presents cardiovascular risks, among others.) “I wish I’d had her when I was forty-five,” Lacob said. At the time, she recalled, she was too depressed to even shower: “I was crying over things all the time, and I had no idea that it was even perimenopause.” Lacob was seated with her legs tucked underneath her on a fluffy couch in the living room of her Mediterranean-style estate. She has a cascade of dark hair, and wore platform Uggs. (At Warriors games, she is often seen on camera dancing in glittery thigh-high boots beside her husband.) “I think I would probably even look ten years younger now if I had had her then,” she said.
paragraphs.zh: 尼科尔·拉科布（Nicole Lacob）——勇士队老板的妻子——在一年多前开始找格林瓦尔德看诊，当时她56岁，正好赶上激素替代疗法启动的时机。（对于停经超过十年的女性，H.R.T.可以提升情绪、性欲和骨骼健康，但它也带来心血管等风险。）“我真希望在我45岁时就遇见她。”拉科布说。那时她回忆说，自己抑郁到连洗澡都不想做：“我每天都在为各种事哭泣，而且根本不知道那是围绝经期。”拉科布当时坐在地中海式庄园客厅的一张绒毛沙发上，双腿并拢并卷在身下。她有一头深色的长发，穿着平台鞋款的UGG雪地靴。（在勇士队比赛中，人们常在镜头里看到她和丈夫并肩穿着闪亮的大腿高筒靴跳舞。）她说：“如果当时我就见到她，我想现在我可能看起来甚至会年轻十岁。”

paragraphs.en: Lacob had always liked the house warm—she used to tease her husband that, if he ever wanted to see her naked, he’d need to keep the heat cranked up. Suddenly, she was always too hot and couldn’t sleep because of night sweats. She’d had breast cancer in her thirties and, consequently, had been warned away from H.R.T., which has been thought to be unsafe for cancer patients. But Greenwald explained that there is only a modest increased risk of recurrence. (This is a controversial position and one that many oncologists oppose, in the belief that data are still emerging and that even a modest risk is not worth taking.) She prescribed an estrogen patch to alleviate the night sweats and an estrogen ring, which is placed inside the vagina and releases local doses of the hormone. “Then Sally asked me questions that I never thought to ask myself, um, regarding desire,” Lacob said.
paragraphs.zh: 拉科布（Lacob）一向喜欢把屋子开得很暖。她以前还笑着调侃丈夫说，如果他想看到她赤裸，就得先把空调温度开高一点。突然之间，她变得总是太热，夜间盗汗让她睡不着。她三十多岁时得过乳腺癌，因此被劝阻不要使用H.R.T.，因为这种疗法被认为对癌症患者不安全。格林瓦尔德解释说，这种做法只会使复发风险略有增加。（这个立场有争议，许多肿瘤科医生也持反对意见，原因是他们认为相关数据仍在不断出现中，即使是小幅上升的风险也不值得承担。）她为她开了雌激素贴片来缓解夜间盗汗，还开了雌激素环，雌激素环放在阴道内，释放局部剂量的激素。‘后来，莎莉问了我一些我从没想过要问自己的问题，尤其是关于欲望这块。’拉科布说。

paragraphs.en: “I was shell-shocked the first time,” Nathalie Dompé, the Italian-born head of a pharmaceutical business and the wife of the tech entrepreneur Chamath Palihapitiya, told me, laughing at the memory of just how graphic Greenwald’s questions can be. “The best part is when I go home and repeat all my new learnings to my husband, who looks at me, shocked.” She appreciated the way Greenwald “puts it on the radar and says, ‘This is part of how you take care of your life.’ ”
paragraphs.zh: “我第一次听到时都被震惊到了。”意大利出生、经营一家制药企业的首席以及科技企业家查玛斯·帕米哈皮蒂亚（Chamath Palihapitiya）的妻子纳塔莉·东普（Nathalie Dompé）对我说，她一边回忆格林瓦尔德的提问有多么直接坦率，一边笑了起来。“最有趣的是，等我回家把这些新发现都复述给丈夫听时，看着他那‘震惊’的表情。’”她很欣赏格林瓦尔德的做法：‘把这些问题摆到台面上说清楚，并且告诉你，这也是你照顾好自己的一部分。’

paragraphs.en: I sat in on one of Greenwald’s appointments, with a patient I’ll call Kristen, the wife of a celebrity. She had been suffering from depression and mood swings triggered by the hormonal fluctuations of perimenopause. She confessed that she sometimes found “the mental load of being a mom”—she has teen-agers—almost unbearable. Greenwald had been treating her for a few years, and they were, Greenwald said, “making progress as a doctor-patient couple.” She had evened out Kristen’s cycles with birth-control pills, and Kristen’s mood and energy had improved. She’d even picked up some old interests again, like exercise and crafting. But not sex.
paragraphs.zh: 我曾旁听过格林瓦尔德的一次会诊，病人我姑且叫她克里斯汀（Kristen），是位名人的妻子。她一直受抑郁和情绪波动困扰，这些症状由围绝经期激素波动引发。她坦言自己有时几乎扛不住“当妈妈的心理负担”——她有一对十几岁的孩子。格林瓦尔德已经给她治疗了几年，格林瓦尔德说，双方作为医生和病人的关系已经“正在取得进展”。她甚至用避孕药帮克里斯汀把月经周期调得更稳定，克里斯汀的情绪和精力都改善了。她还把一些旧兴趣找了回来，比如运动和手工。可谈到性——并没有改善。

paragraphs.en: Greenwald hears this story again and again. After they became mothers, her patients tell her, their focus transferred from their romantic lives to their children. Kristen’s husband’s life was full of glamour and limelight, whereas her life was all behind the scenes. He was taking testosterone to keep up his physique; his libido was stronger than ever. But satisfying him felt like one more chore.
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德一次又一次听到这个故事。她的病人说，做了妈妈之后，重心从浪漫关系转向了孩子。克里斯汀丈夫的生活充满了名利和聚光灯，而她的生活却始终在幕后。丈夫为了维持体型在打睾酮，他的性欲比以往更强。可满足他的需求，对她来说却像是又一项待办清单。

paragraphs.en: Seeing Kristen’s shoulders slump, Greenwald smiled. “When you’re, like, ‘Help a sister out with her sex life,’ know that you’re in good company.” Greenwald said. “Just imagine my very sexy life with my four kids and three jobs!”
paragraphs.zh: 看到Kristen的肩膀耷拉了下来，Greenwald笑了笑：“当你在‘帮姐妹改善性生活’的时候，要知道你不是个例。”Greenwald说。“你不妨先想象一下我那种有四个孩子、还干三份工作的、非常性感的生活。”

paragraphs.en: Kristen said that when she accompanies her husband on work trips without the kids, she feels pressure to be a sex goddess. “I can tell, after the first night, that he’s thinking, That was fun, but no one was swinging from the chandelier,” she said. She missed having “the desire for more spontaneous, fun, crazy sex.” When she was younger, alcohol helped, but these days drinking made her fall asleep more than it loosened her up.“I still like my guy, and I think he’s cute,” she said shyly. “And I want him to only be with me.” But she said that, when he asks if she wants to have sex, it seems that he’s not really asking about her desires. She feels like saying, “No, I don’t. And now you’re annoyed because that was the least sexy answer I could give. But your question was manipulative.”
paragraphs.zh: Kristen说，当她不带孩子跟丈夫出差时，会感到自己有压力，必须像性女神一样表现。“我能在第一晚后就看出来，他在想：那很有意思，但没人从吊灯上荡下来。”她说。她想念的是“更自发、好玩、疯狂一点的性行为”。她年轻时酒精有帮助，但现在喝了酒反而更容易睡着，而不是更放松。“我还是喜欢我那个人，而且我觉得他很可爱，”她害羞地说。“而且我希望他只和我在一起。”但她也说，每次他问她想不想做爱时，他似乎其实没有在问她的真实欲望。她觉得自己像是在说：‘不想。’然后他会不高兴，因为那是她能给出的最不情色的回答。‘而你的问题本身，其实带着操控的意味。’

paragraphs.en: Greenwald, a mother of four boys, preaches the benefits of the efficient five-minute sexual encounter. Rather than “chandelier sex,” she counsels patients to aim for “super-heteronormative, vanilla, white-picket-fence sex that has data behind it.”Photograph by Maggie Shannon for The New Yorker
paragraphs.zh: Greenwald是一个有四个儿子的妈妈，她宣扬高效的五分钟性行为的好处。她建议患者不要追求“吊灯性爱”，而是选择“有数据支持的、超异性规范、香草式、白栅栏般普通”的性行为。照片：Maggie Shannon 摄，刊登于《The New Yorker》

paragraphs.en: Greenwald jumped in, completing Kristen’s thought: “Does it look like I want to have sex? I’m sitting here ordering groceries. What about me ordering groceries says, ‘I want to have sex’?”
paragraphs.zh: Greenwald接着说，接完Kristen的话：“你看，我这会儿是不是在说想要做爱？我现在就在这儿点菜买日用品。我现在点菜买东西，哪里能说明‘我想要做爱’呢？”

paragraphs.en: Then Greenwald made a characteristic pivot. In fact, she always tells patients, any time is a good time to have a certain kind of sex—what she calls “efficient sex.” She believes that the best way to improve sex is to drop the romantic notion that it requires a special frame of mind. Better to have a quickie, even if you’re reluctant, because an orgasm can improve your mood, and ultimately your health and your relationship.
paragraphs.zh: 接着格林瓦尔德做了一个典型的转换。事实上，她总是告诉病人，任何时候都适合进行某种类型的性生活——她把它称作“高效性爱”。她认为，提升性生活的最佳方式是放弃那种浪漫想法，即认为性必须在特定心境下才发生。与其犹豫不前，不如先快速进行一次亲密接触，因为高潮可以改善情绪，最终也能改善你的身心状态和关系。

paragraphs.en: “I’m not going to talk about chandelier sex,” Greenwald added. “That’s a good hobby, and, if you have that, rock on.” She continued, “We’re going to stick to super-heteronormative, vanilla, white-picket-fence sex that has data behind it.” She had suggestions on how Kristen could “curate” her own arousal. Spontaneous desire, she said, disappears in eighty-five per cent of women in partnerships after about eighteen months. But women retain responsive desire—which comes from being stimulated, or from stimulating themselves. “If I were to write a porn for women in our forties, fifties, and sixties, it would start with a vibrator and some lube.” Think of it like exercise, she said: you never want to do it, but, once the trainer arrives and you begin, you get in the mood.
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德补充道：“我不打算谈‘吊灯式性爱’。那倒是个不错的爱好，如果你能做到，就尽管继续。” 她接着说：“我们要坚持的是有数据支持的、超异性化、很普通、白篱笆围栏式的性爱。” 她还给克里斯汀提了如何“筛选”自身性唤起的建议。她说，进入关系后的女性中，大约有百分之八十五在大约十八个月后会失去自发性欲。可女性仍会保有反应性欲望——这种欲望来自被刺激，或是自我刺激。“如果我要给四十、五十、六十岁的人写一本女性向色情作品，它会从震动棒和一些润滑剂开始。” 她说，这就像运动一样：你一开始并不想去做，但一旦教练来了、你开始了，情绪就会被带起来。

paragraphs.en: Kristen nodded: “Before my trainer gets there, I’m, like, Fuck you!”
paragraphs.zh: 克里斯汀点头说：“在我的教练到来之前，我就会说，‘去你的！’”

paragraphs.en: Greenwald went on, “But why do you show up? You’re thinking, Once I start to exercise, I know how good I’ll feel afterward.” She counselled Kristen to hop into bed with a vibrator, then, after warming up, text her husband and ask him to come to the bedroom for a few minutes.
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德继续说道：“那你为什么要出现？你会想：‘一旦我开始锻炼，我就知道事后会有多舒服。’” 她建议克里斯汀先抱着震动棒上床，等暖身之后，再发短信给丈夫，让他来卧室待上几分钟。

paragraphs.en: “I know that you’re, like, That doesn’t feel sexy,” Greenwald said. “But it’s showing up at the party, even if you don’t think you want to go, and then having a good time.” She continued, “I know it’s awkward in the MeToo era.” She realizes that her message—“Just try it, even though you don’t want to”—can sound retrograde, misconstrued as an update on “Close your eyes and think of England.” But, for a middle-aged woman in “a committed relationship with a nice partner,” she believes that it’s a sound approach to improving a couple’s sex life.
paragraphs.zh: “我知道你会说：‘这听起来不性感，’格林沃尔德说，‘但你要去到场，哪怕你一开始不想去，而且还要玩得开心。’她接着说：‘我知道在 #MeToo 时代会很尴尬。’她意识到自己的这句劝告——‘试着去做，即使你不想要也先试试’——听上去可能是倒退的，也可能被误解成像‘闭上眼睛想象英格兰’那种鸡汤式暗示。可是她认为，对于一个与“好伴侣”处于“稳定关系”的中年女性来说，这是改善夫妻性生活的一个可行方法。”

paragraphs.en: Greenwald told Kristen that she needed to do some cognitive reframing. “Instead of asking, ‘Do you want me?’ ”—an epic question, requiring an epic answer—“couples should practice asking, ‘Hey, do you have five minutes?’ ” Sex, Greenwald often says, begets more sex. Her date-night tip: when out to dinner with their husbands, women should carry some travel-sized lube and pop into the ladies’ room to apply it right before dessert. “It’s hard to walk around with a wet vagina and not feel turned on,” she said.
paragraphs.zh: 格林沃尔德对克里斯汀说，她需要做的是一种认知重构。“你们应该把问题从‘你想要我吗？’——一个需要史诗级回答的问题——改成‘嘿，你有五分钟吗？’”她常说，性生活会带来更多的性生活。她给晚餐约会的建议是：妻子在和丈夫外出吃饭时，带一支袖珍润滑液，等上洗手间时趁机在甜点上桌前涂上去。她说：‘带着湿润的阴道到处走却不觉得兴奋，这很难。’

paragraphs.en: One of Greenwald’s strategies is getting patients to practice inwardly narrating the sequence leading up to sex: He’s rubbing my back. I’m breathing a little faster. I feel warm. The goal is to banish the more common inner-mom monologue: He’s touching me. Did I remember those snacks for tomorrow? How will my daughter feel if no one asks her to prom? As with meditation, Greenwald said, patients gain control over their thoughts: “If it’s a Tuesday night before sports pickup, this is how you create good, efficient bedroom sex with your spouse that doesn’t involve a whip and leather boots.”
paragraphs.zh: 格林沃尔德的一项策略，是让病人开始在心里复盘性行为前的过程：他在抚摸我的背，我的呼吸有点快了，我觉得发热。这样做的目的是驱逐更常见的“内在妈妈式独白”：他在摸我。明天我记得给孩子带小吃吗？如果没人请她去舞会她会怎么想？格林沃尔德说，和冥想一样，病人可以逐渐掌控自己的想法：‘如果这是个周二晚，孩子有体育活动要接，你可以这样为和伴侣在床上创造一次高效又顺畅的性爱，而且不需要鞭子和皮靴。’

paragraphs.en: To keep the routine going, she advises couples to replace postcoital sweet nothings with compliments like “I love how efficient we were!” The idea is to tap into “that reward system where you have sex, you sleep well, you wake up more energized, you guys kiss before he goes off to work, you feel more loved by him, and the next night happens.” Describing why Greenwald’s methods work, Nathalie Dompé reached for an Italian phrase, “La fame vien mangiando”—the hunger comes while you’re eating.
paragraphs.zh: 为了让这个流程持续下去，她建议夫妻把事后甜言蜜语换成称赞，例如“我喜欢我们这次效率这么高！”核心是触发“你做爱了、睡得好、醒来更有活力、你们在他去上班前再接一个吻、你更感到被他爱着，而下一晚又会发生”这种奖赏机制。纳塔莉·东佩在解释格林沃尔德方法为何有效时用了一个意大利短语：“La fame vien mangiando”——边吃边饿。

paragraphs.en: It’s no surprise that expensive concierge medical care is better. It’s a particularly distressing example of how, in our society, necessities are becoming luxuries. Concierge care is innately problematic; when the wealthy can buy their way out of public-health problems, it reduces pressure to fix them. Gynecologists typically book fifteen-minute appointments: five minutes to do a breast exam and a Pap smear, five minutes to talk, and five minutes to document the visit. Greenwald gives patients as much time as they need, often an hour or two per visit.
paragraphs.zh: 昂贵的“私人医疗”并不奇怪地更有质量。它恰恰是不禁让人不安的一个例子：在我们的社会里，必需品正在变成奢侈品。私人会诊制医疗本身就有先天问题；当富人可以花钱躲开公共卫生问题时，社会就更少有动力去真正解决这些问题。妇科医生通常会安排十五分钟的门诊：五分钟做乳房检查和巴氏涂片，五分钟与患者交谈，五分钟记录病历。格林瓦尔德会让患者有足够时间，通常每次就诊能聊上一小时到两小时。

paragraphs.en: “I think the system is broken,” she told me. Greenwald’s medical career was a form of response, stemming from her own experiences. When the COVID-19 pandemic hit, she felt herself beginning to crack.
paragraphs.zh: “我觉得这个体系是坏的，”她对我说。格林瓦尔德的医学职业生涯本身就是一种回应，源于她个人的经历。新冠疫情爆发时，她感到自己开始崩溃。

paragraphs.en: Growing up near Stanford, in a close-knit family, as the granddaughter of a Stanford doctor, Greenwald had a vision of herself being an ob-gyn at the university—delivering babies at the same hospital where she was born—and having four sons. She became chief resident at the ob-gyn program at the University of California, San Francisco, and married a pediatrician, Daniel Imler. Throughout the eighty-hour workweeks of the residency, where she was permitted to take off only a month after the birth of her first son, she found herself going against protocol to co-sleep with the baby, because it was the only time she saw him. Passionate about health equity, she did a master’s degree in public health at Tufts, but she became disenchanted with the population-based approach it taught: she saw suboptimal high-volume care, in which the health of the individual was sacrificed to cost considerations, everywhere.
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德在斯坦福附近长大，出身关系紧密的家庭，是一位斯坦福医生的孙女，她设想过自己将成为该大学的妇产科医生，在自己出生的同一家医院接生，还拥有四个儿子。她在加州大学旧金山分校妇产科项目担任主任住院医，并嫁给了儿科医生丹尼尔·艾姆勒。她在每周八十小时的住院医生训练中度过了很长一段时间，在那里她只允许在第一个儿子出生后请假一个月；她因此常常违反规定与孩子同床而睡，因为那是她能见到他唯一的时机。她热衷于健康公平，曾在塔夫茨大学攻读公共卫生硕士学位，但她对该课程所教授的人群导向方法很快失去了热情：她发现到处都存在一种低质量的高吞吐量医疗，在这种模式下，个人健康常常被牺牲于成本考量之下。

paragraphs.en: Medicine suited her personality. “I’m a little hyper, driven, anxious,” Greenwald told me. “But it works in medicine, because I am always worrying about excellence, perseverating on my patients.” In a public-health setting, that was the wrong mind-set. Only easy patients got good care, because time-consuming workups or expensive tests—let alone innovation—were prohibitive.
paragraphs.zh: 医学很适合她的性格。“我有点偏高能、很有冲劲，也比较焦虑，”格林瓦尔德对我说。“但这在医学里是有用的，因为我总是担心自己是否做到最好，会反复思考每一个患者。”在公共卫生体系里，这恰恰是错误的思维模式。只有“容易处理”的患者才能获得好的治疗，因为耗时的检查、昂贵的检验——更别说创新——都代价太高。

paragraphs.en: At thirty-two, Greenwald joined a Stanford health-care practice made up of four women and a man in Menlo Park. There, she saw up to twenty-five patients a day. She and her partners were perpetually overworked, and there was tension in the group because they had to cover for one another if they or their kids were sick. Back-to-back overnights left Greenwald exhausted and depressed.
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德三十二岁时，加入了位于门洛帕克的斯坦福一家医疗团队。该团队由四名女性和一名男性医生组成。那时，她每天最多接诊二十五名患者。她和合伙人长期超负荷工作，而且团队内关系紧张，因为一旦有人或他们的孩子生病，就必须互相顶班。连续通宵值班让格林瓦尔德身心俱疲，情绪低落。

paragraphs.en: When she was thirty-six weeks pregnant with her second child, Greenwald was in the middle of a seventy-two-hour shift—subsisting on saltines and cranberry juice between deliveries—when she went into premature labor. “He went home at four pounds,” she recalled, of her son. When COVID began, she discovered that she was pregnant again. Wearing an N95 mask so that she could nap in the hospital during patients’ long labors, she was anxious about her fetus.
paragraphs.zh: 当她怀着第二个孩子已满三十六周时，格林瓦尔德正在进行一场长达七十二小时的轮班——在一次次分娩之间靠苏打饼干和蔓越莓汁度日——结果却早产。她回忆说，儿子“降生时只有四磅”。新冠疫情爆发时，她又发现自己再次怀孕了。为了在患者漫长分娩期间能在医院小憩，她戴着N95口罩，但她始终为胎儿的情况感到焦虑。

paragraphs.en: At one point, the head of the practice asked Greenwald to pay special attention to a wealthy patient, and so, for once, she was able to practice medicine the way she wanted to, spending extra time on the case and catching issues that might have gone unnoticed. Greenwald wasn’t able to give herself, or her regular patients, that level of care. After overnight deliveries, she would find herself driving home too fast in order to get there in time for her husband to leave for work; she would remind him to give the boys the breakfast she had prepped the night before and to get them to preschool, and then she’d try to squeeze in a twenty-minute nap before returning to the office. One day in 2022, she fell asleep at a red light with her boys in the back seat and finally decided to quit her job. But she needed to work, and she still wanted to practice medicine. By then, her husband was the medical director of Stanford’s Pediatric Emergency Department, but they had little savings. Their money had gone into a cozy Craftsman house in Menlo Park, and repaying medical-school debt and the salary for their nanny ate up the majority of Greenwald’s take-home pay.
paragraphs.zh: 有一次，诊所负责人要求格林瓦尔德特别照顾一位富有患者，因此这才得以按她理想的方式行医，在这个病例上多花时间并发现了否则可能被忽视的问题。她无法把这种照护水平同样用于自己或平常患者。夜里接生后，她常常开得很快赶回家，以便及时接送丈夫上班；她会提醒丈夫给她昨晚已经准备好的早餐喂给两个孩子，并把他们送去学前班，然后在回诊室前尽量挤出二十分钟小睡。2022年的一天，她在红灯前和孩子们一起在车里睡着了，最终下定决心辞职。但她又需要收入，也依然想继续行医。到那时，她丈夫已成斯坦福儿童急诊科的医疗主任，但他们积蓄不多。大部分钱都投入了位于门洛帕克的一栋舒适木结构（Craftsman）房子，还款、以及支付医学院债务和保姆工资也吃掉了格林瓦尔德大部分的税后收入。

paragraphs.en: Greenwald decided to open her own concierge gynecology practice with no obstetrics, initially setting a thousand dollars a year as a membership fee. Hers was the only such practice in the area, and she didn’t know if it would work. On the side, she consulted for primary-care concierge practices and spent three days a month at Santa Clara Valley Medical Center, teaching and delivering babies for underserved populations, a commitment that she has continued.
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德决定开设一家不提供产科服务的私人订制妇科诊所，并最初把年费定为一千美元。这是该地区唯一一家此类诊所，她也并不确定是否能成功。顺便，她还为初级保健的私人订制诊所提供咨询，并每月在圣克拉拉谷医疗中心工作三天，为医疗资源不足的群体授课和接生，这一承诺她一直在坚持。

paragraphs.en: At her own practice, she decided that patients would never wait, that she would be available from 6 A.M. to 9 P.M., and that she would never have to cancel because she was delivering a baby. The office would provide spa-fluffy robes, heated towels, and premium teas. She would answer texts and calls at all hours, including on weekends, and would travel to patients’ pool houses for a quick infection check or meet them on the tarmac when they flew, in a private jet, up from L.A.
paragraphs.zh: 在她自己的诊所里，她决定患者永远不需要等待，自己从上午6点到晚上9点都保持接诊状态，并且不会因为要接生婴儿而临时取消。诊所会提供像水疗会所那样的宽大长袍、加热毛巾和高级茶饮。她会在任何时间段回复短信和电话，包括周末；也会开车到病人的泳池别墅做快速感染检查，或者在他们乘坐私人飞机从洛杉矶飞来时，在机场停机坪迎接他们。

paragraphs.en: Greenwald obtained medical licenses in New York and Florida so that she could take care of patients’ extended families. She sees her patients’ adolescent daughters, arranging anesthesia for IUD placements (prescribed to treat painful periods)—something that is rarely done, even though the insertion can be excruciating. She advises inexperienced teen-agers that lubes containing additives are dehydrating and create micro-abrasions that can increase the transmission of S.T.D.s, and she helps them navigate hookup culture, in which choking, polyamory, and anal sex have become mainstream. Ella Hale, a recent college grad who started seeing Greenwald at fifteen, told me, “She said, ‘If you feel any psychological or physical discomfort during sex, I want to know.’ ”
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德在纽约和佛罗里达都拿到了医疗执照，这样她就能照顾患者的大家庭成员。她接诊患者的青春期女儿，并为她们安排宫内节育器（IUD）植入时的麻醉，这通常不被许多医生做，尽管这个插入过程可能非常疼痛。她会劝告缺乏经验的青少年：含有添加剂的润滑剂会使身体脱水，还会造成微小摩擦伤，从而增加性传播疾病（STD）的传播风险；并帮助她们在“钩搭文化”中周全应对，在这种文化里，掐喉、多人关系和肛交已成为主流。埃拉·海尔（Ella Hale）——一名最近的大学毕业生，十五岁就开始看格林瓦尔德——告诉我：“她曾经说过：‘如果你在性行为中有任何心理或身体上的不适，我希望你告诉我。’”

paragraphs.en: Greenwald tracks all of her patients’ metrics: blood tests to check that hormones are balanced; Oura rings to show resting heart rates and sleep scores; glucose monitors to keep tabs on blood sugar; food-tracking apps for logging daily protein targets. She sends them to get thousand-dollar Grail blood tests (the fact that they are not covered by insurance is not an issue for the B.V.C.), which may detect cancer years before it appears on any scan, and full-body MRIs (two thousand dollars), and she reminds them to pick up sunscreen on vacations abroad, because European formulations are more effective than what we can get here. Because there is heart disease in Gisel Kordestani’s family, Greenwald referred her to a preventative cardiologist who had her take the Cleerly cardiovascular test, an A.I.-based analysis of heart CT scans which yields new insights. Kordestani told me that she didn’t care about wasting time or money: “Now I know my cardio risk is zero.”
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德会追踪所有患者的各项指标：抽血检测激素是否平衡；用 Oura 手环记录静息心率和睡眠分数；用血糖监测器持续观察血糖；用食物追踪应用记录每日蛋白质目标。她建议她们做一千美元的 Grail 血液检测（这些检查不被保险报销，这对“B.V.C.”来说并不是问题），这类检测可在癌症在任何影像学检查上显现之前多年被发现；她还安排她们做全身 MRI，价格约两千美元；并提醒她们出国度假时要买防晒霜，因为欧洲的配方更有效，这是我们这里买不到的。由于吉赛尔·科德斯塔尼（Gisel Kordestani）家族有心脏病史，格林瓦尔德把她转介给一位预防医学心脏科医生，让她做了 Cleerly 心血管测试，这是一种基于人工智能分析心脏 CT 的筛查，可带来新的医学见解。科德斯塔尼告诉我，她根本不在乎浪费时间或金钱：“现在我知道我的心血管风险是零了。”

paragraphs.en: Greenwald focussed on optimizing her own well-being, too, waking at four-thirty to work out, and then answering patient e-mails while sipping bone broth or water mixed with psyllium husk (for fibre) and creatine. She attended conferences and pored over the latest research.
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德也把自己健康的优化放在同等重要的位置，她每天清晨四点半起床去锻炼，然后一边喝骨头高汤或掺了车前子壳粉（补纤维）和肌酸的水，一边回复患者邮件。她还会参加会议，仔细研读最新研究。

paragraphs.en: She had a fourth son, and her time felt more valuable than ever. She raised her prices a few times. Consultations became a thousand dollars, then two thousand. She decided to accept new members with tiered annual fees, starting at ten thousand dollars and exceeding thirty thousand for the most extensive care, which can involve assembling a specialized care team to monitor complex comorbidities. Then she stopped accepting the ten-thousand-dollar patients. What was the market price for this kind of care, she wondered. The influencer and doctor Peter Attia has a longevity practice with patients who pay six-figure membership fees. Clearly, there was room to keep going up.
paragraphs.zh: 她又生了第四个儿子，而且她的时间似乎前所未有地有价值。她几次提高了价格，咨询费从一千美元涨到两千美元。她决定接收新的会员，按年度层级收费，起步一万美元，最全面的护理则超过三万美元，这种护理可能需要组建一支专门的团队来监测复杂的共病情况。随后她又停止接收一万美元会员。她心想，这类护理的市场价究竟该是多少？网红兼医生彼得·阿蒂亚（Peter Attia）拥有一项长寿诊所，患者支付六位数的会员费。显然，这种模式里还存在继续抬升的空间。

paragraphs.en: One afternoon, I sat in on another appointment of Greenwald’s, this time with a patient in her fifties who had been diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer. The woman, whom I’ll call Lily, was agonizing between having a lumpectomy, which would entail taking the estrogen-blocking drug tamoxifen for five years, or getting a double mastectomy. Her oncologists were hedging, telling her that it was a personal choice. She knew that tamoxifen would curdle her sex life by inducing abrupt menopause; she was already struggling with symptoms of perimenopause, which her primary-care doctor had dissuaded her from treating.
paragraphs.zh: 一个下午，我又旁听了格林沃尔德的一次门诊。这次的病人是位五十多岁的女性，已被确诊为早期乳腺癌。她——我姑且叫她莉莉——在选择保乳手术和双侧乳房切除之间进退两难；前者意味着她需要服用他莫昔芬五年。她的肿瘤科医生在建议中故意保留余地，告诉她这是个人选择。她知道他莫昔芬会在她体内迅速引发停经样反应，坏掉她的性趣和性生活，而且她本身已经在与围绝经期症状作斗争，但她的家庭医生曾劝阻她去治疗这些症状。

paragraphs.en: Lily had an active life of golfing, skiing, and boating with her family. In the examining room, she sat on the table with Dior sunglasses perched on her head, wearing a ruffled, checked blouse and jeans—a California trad-wife look. Greenwald got her talking about her life, and her hopes for grandchildren one day. After a moment’s thought, Greenwald told her to do the lumpectomy—and the tamoxifen, which would cut the chance of cancer recurrence in half. She surprised Lily by recommending that she simultaneously do hormone therapy—a vaginal application, such as estradiol cream—to mitigate her symptoms. “Take some tamoxifen and know that, if you need a partner to respond to whatever shitty symptoms you’re dealing with, I’ve got you,” she said. “We’ll be creative.” She offered to send Lily a new academic paper on H.R.T. and cancer risk. If Lily ended up losing her sunny disposition, Greenwald said, they could explore solutions, such as Duavee, an estrogen replacement now in trial with high-risk women; contrary to conventional wisdom, emerging data seem to indicate that this form of estrogen is associated with a decrease in breast cancer.
paragraphs.zh: 莉莉的生活很丰富：打高尔夫、滑雪、和家人一起划船。门诊检查室里，她坐在诊台上，头上戴着迪奥太阳镜，上身穿着荷叶边格子衬衫和牛仔裤——有点加州“传统家庭主妇”式穿搭。格林沃尔德让她聊起了生活，也聊起了她对将来能否抱到孙辈的期待。想了片刻后，格林沃尔德建议她做保乳手术，并加用他莫昔芬，因为这可以把癌症复发的几率减半。她令莉莉意外的是，还建议她同步进行激素治疗——比如用雌二醇乳膏这种阴道局部用药——来缓解症状。“先吃他莫昔芬，同时知道如果你需要有人帮你应对正在经历的那些糟糕症状，我会在你这边。”她说，“我们会找到办法。”她还愿意把一篇关于激素替代疗法与癌症风险的新学术论文发给莉莉。格林沃尔德说，如果莉莉最后失去了那种开朗向上的状态，他们可以再探索其他方案，比如杜阿维（Duavee），这是一种正在对高风险女性进行试验中的雌激素替代疗法；与传统观点相反，正在出现的数据似乎表明，这种形式的雌激素与乳腺癌风险降低相关。

paragraphs.en: “Duavee is blowing my freaking mind,” Greenwald said.
paragraphs.zh: “杜阿维真是让我震惊。”格林沃尔德说。

paragraphs.en: “Am I a good dog who sometimes does bad things or a bad dog who sometimes does good things?”
paragraphs.zh: “我是那种有时会做坏事却又算好狗，还是有时会做好事的坏狗呢？”

paragraphs.en: Cartoon by Amy Hwang
paragraphs.zh: 插图作者：Amy Hwang

paragraphs.en: While mainstream oncology supports using local vaginal hormones that have little systemic absorption (although some doctors remain reluctant), the use of systemic hormones for women who have had breast cancer is contentious. Even for those who haven’t, hormone therapy is “a Pandora’s box,” Mark Moasser, a U.C.S.F. oncologist and cancer researcher, told me. “It remains a field with different data sets and studies and different opinions and judgments. There shouldn’t be a ‘one size fits all’ guideline. It’s a matter of judgment between the doctor and the patient. I can’t say it’s ‘safe’ because we simply don’t know.”
paragraphs.zh: 虽然主流肿瘤学支持使用局部阴道激素，因为其全身吸收很少（尽管一些医生仍然持保留态度），但对乳腺癌患者使用全身性激素则颇具争议。即使是对未患乳腺癌的女性，激素治疗也是“潘多拉的盒子”，加州大学旧金山分校（U.C.S.F.）的肿瘤科医生兼癌症研究员马克·莫瑟（Mark Moasser）告诉我。“这仍是一个充满不同数据集、不同研究、不同观点与判断的领域。这里不该有‘一刀切’的指南。它应当由医生与患者共同判断。我要说它‘安全’也不敢，因为我们根本还不知道。”

paragraphs.en: Having dealt with Lily’s cancer questions, Greenwald pivoted to her sex life. Was it A+? Greenwald suddenly included me in the conversation, breezily asking, “Do you two have internal orgasms?” Lily and I both looked at her, agog. Greenwald has a crisp, matter-of-fact manner. With her practiced explanations and gestures, she could almost be a flight attendant explaining preflight safety protocols.
paragraphs.zh: 在回答了莉莉的癌症问题后，格林瓦尔德把话题转向了她的性生活。她的性生活是 A+ 吗？格林瓦尔德突然把我拉进了对话，随意地问道：“你们俩有阴道内射精吗？”莉莉和我都惊讶地看着她。格林瓦尔德的语气干脆、务实。凭借她熟练的解释和手势，几乎可以想象成一位空姐在讲解登机前的安全演示。

paragraphs.en: Only one in five women climax through internal stimulation alone, Greenwald said. Many never find the mysterious G-spot. But the G-spot becomes increasingly important in middle age, because women can’t rely on their old sexual responses to stay the same. “The clitoral nerve has two different nerve fibres, right?” Greenwald went on, as Lily and I nodded dumbly. This nerve root runs both internally and externally, ending in the clitoral nub, and it contains two different types of fibres, one of which responds to light touch and warmth, and another that senses vibration and pressure. But as we age, she noted, the fibre that responds to light touch and heat degrades, whereas the one that senses vibration and pressure maintains sensitivity. After menopause, with insufficient estrogen to nourish the tissue and facilitate blood flow, the clitoris starts to lose its sensitivity, resulting in poor-quality orgasms. (I had never heard of poor-quality orgasms.)
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德表示，只有五分之一的女性仅靠阴道内刺激就能达到高潮。许多人从未真正找到那神秘的 G 点。但在中年，G 点会变得越来越重要，因为女性不能再依赖年轻时那套性反应模式。她接着说：“阴蒂神经有两种不同的神经纤维，对吧？”莉莉和我都愣住了。这个神经根既向内也向外延伸，末端位于阴蒂小丘，并包含两类不同的纤维：一种对轻触和温度敏感，另一种则感知振动和压力。但是她指出，随着年龄增长，负责轻触和热感的纤维会退化，而负责振动和压力的纤维则能保持敏感性。更年期后，体内雌激素不足，无法为组织提供营养并促进血流，阴蒂便开始失去敏感度，导致高潮质量变差。（我以前从未听说过“低质量高潮”这个概念。）

paragraphs.en: “So you’re going to come in here at some point, be it on your tamoxifen journey or when you’re eighty-five, and say, ‘We’ve done this position for forty years, and now it isn’t working.’ ” Greenwald counsels women to practice finding the G-spot and stimulate the deep internal nerve fibres that remain robust.
paragraphs.zh: “所以总有一天，你会来这里，无论是在接受他莫昔芬治疗的过程中，还是在八十五岁的时候，说：‘我们做了这个姿势四十年，可是现在不行了。’”格林瓦尔德劝女性练习寻找 G 点，去刺激那些仍然稳健的深部阴道内神经纤维。

paragraphs.en: “Do you have a vibrator?” she asked Lily. The answer was an enthusiastic yes, and Greenwald offered to provide instructions for exercises to try at home. If the G-spot proved elusive, Lily should come back and Greenwald would show her where it is. Wrapping up, Greenwald said, with feeling, “I support you and your energy and your sex life and your orgasm quality and your vagina and your pelvic floor.” She looked Lily in the eye to make sure she heard it. Her parting words: “I’m on your team.”
paragraphs.zh: “你有振动棒吗？”她问莉莉。莉莉很热情地回答有，格林瓦尔德于是提出要给她一些可在家里尝试的练习指导。若是 G 点依旧难以定位，莉莉应当回来，格林瓦尔德会当面示范告诉她它的位置。结束时，格林瓦尔德满含感情地说：“我支持你，支持你的精力、你的性生活、你的高潮质量、你的阴道和你的盆底。”她直视莉莉的眼睛，确保她听见了。她的告别语是：“我站在你这边。”

paragraphs.en: With four boys under the age of nine, Greenwald has organized her family life so that it functions like a well-run startup. One night, she invited me to her house for dinner. Wearing sweatpants and a T-shirt, she jiggled her plump baby and showed me a mudroom where each of the boys has a cubby near a refrigerator that holds their lunches. “I am obsessed with systems,” she said. Outside the mudroom is a large touch screen that her sons are supposed to stop at and check off tasks on a schedule (“Eat breakfast and clear dishes.” “Sit on toilet and count to 30!”); each time, the screen rewards them with an explosion of confetti. Greenwald coaches the boys on social behavior: “Look at Melanie when you talk! Can she see your eyeballs?”
paragraphs.zh: 格林瓦尔德有四个九岁以下的孩子，她把家庭生活安排得像一家运转良好的创业公司。一个晚上，她邀请我到她家吃晚饭。她穿着运动裤和 T 恤，怀里抱着胖乎乎的婴儿，还带我看她的杂物间，那里每个儿子在冰箱旁边都有一个小储物柜，装着他们的午餐。“我对系统很着迷，”她说。杂物间外面有一块大触摸屏，儿子们应当在上面停下来，并按计划勾选任务（“吃早餐并清理餐具。”“上厕所后数到三十！”）；每完成一项，屏幕就会放出一阵五彩纸屑奖励。格林瓦尔德还会对孩子们的社交行为进行指导：“和梅拉妮说话时看着她！她能看到你的眼珠吗？”

paragraphs.en: The house is colorful, with wallpaper covered in giant leaping zebras. One of her sons showed me his pet rabbit; another introduced a large parrot squawking in a cage. There is also a lizard, four hermit crabs, a toothed frog, and a wall-size fish tank that the kids built with their father; its filtration system is based on the workings of a kidney. Greenwald’s husband had just returned from a monthlong stint in Rwanda, teaching pediatric urgent care. Their nine-year-old had joined him, playing the role of patient. Like his wife, Imler is plainspoken and believes in cutting to the chase. “Sometimes I hear doctors cloak the news of a child’s death in so much flowery language that the parents don’t grasp what’s happened,” he told me.
paragraphs.zh: 这幢房子色彩斑斓，墙纸上印着一只只巨大的腾跃斑马。她的一个儿子给我看了他的宠物兔子；另一个儿子给我介绍了一只关在笼子里大声吵闹的鹦鹉。家里还养了蜥蜴、四只寄居蟹、一只齿蛙，还有一个像墙面一样大的鱼缸，是孩子们和他们爸爸一起搭建的，过滤系统仿照肾脏的运作方式设计。Greenwald 的丈夫刚从卢旺达结束一个月的执教回国，期间他在教儿科急诊护理。九岁的儿子也跟着去了，扮演了病人的角色。和妻子一样，Imler 说话很直接，主张直入主题。“有时我听到医生用很多华丽的辞藻来转移一个孩子去世的消息，结果父母反而听不懂到底发生了什么。”

paragraphs.en: Imler said that he admired how Greenwald likes to talk about “difficult things with people because it feels real”; he is amused by what he described as her impatience with fiction and art. “Her response to ‘Phantom of the Opera’ is ‘There’s a phantom—why are we singing about it for hours? Put him in jail!’ ” He thinks that part of her appeal to patients is that she is unfazed by their net worth. “Sally will not bow to you,” he said. “And people love that.”
paragraphs.zh: Imler 说他很欣赏 Greenwald 喜欢和别人谈“困难的话题，因为这才真实”，并且他对她对虚构文学和艺术表现出的不耐烦感到好笑。“她对《歌剧魅影》的反应是：‘有个幽灵，为什么要为了它唱上好几个小时？把他关进监狱吧！’”他认为吸引病人的一部分原因，是她从不在意对方的资产净值。“Sally 不会对你俯首称臣，”他说，“而人们正是喜欢这个。”

paragraphs.en: Greenwald regularly gets together at a restaurant with concierge-doctor friends. At a recent gathering, over drinks and meze, they talked about the coolest new things they are doing for their patients. Justin Lotfi, a primary-care physician to a fortunate few, offered that he’d been experimenting with a nasal procedure using an anesthetic that, with repeated applications, can break a cycle of migraines.
paragraphs.zh: Greenwald 经常和一群 concierge 医生朋友在餐厅聚会。最近一次聚会上，他们在喝酒和吃 meze 时，讨论了最近给病人提供的最前沿做法。Justin Lotfi——一位只服务于少数幸运客户的全科医生——提到，他一直在尝试一种鼻腔治疗，使用一种麻醉药，反复应用后可以打破偏头痛的循环。

paragraphs.en: “I love being in concierge medicine, because practicing at the top 0.001 per cent—that’s where innovation happens,” Greenwald told me. “I get to sit around and think about these things.” She went on, “I am always, like, ‘What’s next? What’s better? Why don’t we try this?’ ” She added, “But at some point the system needs to evolve so other people can access this kind of care.”
paragraphs.zh: “我喜欢从事 concierge medicine，因为在前 0.001% 的领域行医——创新就在那儿发生，”Greenwald 对我说。“我可以坐下来好好想这些问题。”她接着说：“我总是在想，‘下一步是什么？更好的方法是什么？我们为什么不试试这个？’”她补充道：“但到某个时候，这个体系必须进化，让更多人能够获得这种护理。”

paragraphs.en: Midi Health, a virtual-care platform for women in menopause and perimenopause which was founded in 2021, offers the same sorts of hormone treatments that Greenwald does, including options for cancer patients, and it accepts insurance. Joanna Strober, the entrepreneur behind Midi, supports Greenwald’s approach, but told me, “I am sad that women believe they have to pay all that money to get menopause care.” She added that Midi does most everything that Greenwald does “without the in-person component.” When Strober was raising money to start Midi, male venture capitalists told her that menopause was “niche”; now Midi has a billion-dollar valuation.
paragraphs.zh: Midi Health 是一家面向更年期和围绝经期女性的虚拟护理平台，成立于2021年，提供与 Greenwald 相同类型的激素治疗方案，包括面向癌症患者的选项，并且接受保险报销。Midi 的创业者乔安娜·斯特罗伯（Joanna Strober）支持 Greenwald 的做法，但她对我说：“我很难过，因为女性觉得必须为获得更年期护理而支付那么多钱。”她补充道，Midi 几乎把 Greenwald 所做的一切都做到位了，而且“没有面对面就诊环节”。在斯特罗伯筹集 Midi 创始资金时，男性风险投资人曾告诉她更年期只是“细分市场”；如今 Midi 已达到十亿美元估值。

paragraphs.en: To help close the gap in medical care, Greenwald is trying to publicize her ideas, through her book and public speaking. Last fall, she was a guest on Peter Attia’s popular medical-and-life-style podcast, “The Drive.” When she got to the importance of lube for vaginal health, Attia pushed back, repeatedly questioning whether young women needed lube, since reduced friction might detract from a man’s enjoyment of sex. Greenwald held firm. The episode ended up having “The Drive” ’s highest ever Day One-download count on Apple metrics. Three months later, when a batch of files about Jeffrey Epstein was released by the Department of Justice, Attia’s name turned up more than seventeen hundred times. In one bit of banter from 2016, Attia e-mailed Epstein, “Pussy is, indeed, low carb. Still awaiting results on gluten content, though.” In another exchange, Epstein muses to Attia that he’s not sure why “women live past reproductive age at all.”
paragraphs.zh: 为弥补医疗照护的差距，Greenwald 正在通过自己的书籍和公开演讲传播这些观点。去年秋天，她曾作为嘉宾出席彼得·阿蒂亚（Peter Attia）人气很高的医学与生活方式播客《The Drive》。当谈及阴道健康中润滑剂的重要性时，阿蒂亚提出反对，反复质疑年轻女性是否需要润滑剂，因为减少摩擦可能会降低男性对性行为的享受。Greenwald 坚持己见。该期节目最终创下了 Apple 平台上《The Drive》历史上“Day One”下载量最高的纪录。三个月后，当司法部公布一批关于杰弗里·爱泼斯坦（Jeffrey Epstein）的文件时，阿蒂亚的名字在其中出现超过一千七百次。2016年的一段玩笑式邮件中，阿蒂亚给爱泼斯坦写道：“确实，‘Pussy’是低碳的。不过我还在等检测结果，看它是否含麸质。”在另一段往来中，爱泼斯坦向阿蒂亚感叹，他不明白“女性为什么要活到生育年龄之外。”

paragraphs.en: After the Epstein news broke, the Post published a snarky piece about Greenwald’s episode of “The Drive,” and internet critics piled on. Greenwald declined to comment on Attia, with whom she shares patients, but said that she is eager to engage with the manosphere, including on platforms associated with toxic masculinity. That, she said, would be “a great way for me to push this agenda to an audience that is not typically listening. If there are chances to spread my message that sexual health is health, I’ll take that opportunity every time.” ♦
paragraphs.zh: 在爱泼斯坦事件爆发后，《华盛顿邮报》刊登了一篇带有讽刺口吻的文章，内容聚焦 Greenwald 在《The Drive》上的那期节目，网络评论也随即蜂拥而上。Greenwald 拒绝对与她共用患者资源的阿蒂亚发表评论，但她表示自己非常愿意与“男子圈”对话，包括在与有毒男性气质相关的平台上发声。她说，这将是“把我的议题推向通常不听这类内容的受众的好机会。”她补充说，“只要有机会向公众传播性健康就是健康这个信息，我会每次都抓住。” ♦

paragraphs.en: An earlier version of this article inaccurately contextualized a quotation from Mark Moasser.
paragraphs.zh: 该文早期版本在表述马克·莫瑟（Mark Moasser）的一句引言时，提供了不准确的背景说明。
